algorithm: The designed algorithm dynamically prioritizes customers based on the urgency of their time windows, relative distance, and potential wait times, iteratively updating truck routes to minimize overall last truck completion time while adapting to new customer additions.

This new algorithm calculates the incremental load balancing factor among all trucks by assessing how evenly visitations are distributed, and integrates this factor into the selection process along with time windows and direct travel costs to choose the next destination.

The algorithm evaluates each unvisited customer by calculating a cost function that includes the direct travel distance, a waiting or delay penalty based on customer availability, and an energy consumption estimate derived from distance traveled, aiming to minimize environmental impact and operational costs.

The algorithm dynamically evaluates and selects the next node for a truck based on minimizing a heuristic of combined travel time, adherence to customer availability windows, and penalty for potential delays, improving responsiveness by adapting to the addition of new customers and their time constraints.

My new algorithm utilizes a scoring function which not only prioritizes urgency of customer time windows and travel distance, but also considers the collective route efficiency by influencing decisions based on the proximity of all other trucks and weights the penalty more heavily for missing windows towards the end of the route.
